title: Net profit in Chitina
summary: O 'BRIEN CREEK -- The line to Mother Nature's grocery forms here sometime before 5 a.m. With the far north sun offering plenty of light but little heat, people shiver in the queue beneath leafy cottonwood trees to await the first of the day's charter boats that will haul them down the Copper River into Wood Canyon to pursue salmon. Off to the east, the river is a brown, glacial slurry rushing toward the Gulf of Alaska. More than a week before, the salmon -- sleek reds and big, powerful kings -- entered its mouth nearly 150 miles to the south. For days, the fish have been battling their way blindly upstream toward spawning grounds even farther on. Hundreds of thousands of these fish will eventually make it there to spawn and die. Tens of thousands will not. Their struggles will end in someo…

title: A swim through pike-infested Nancy Lakes
summary: ANNE RAUP / Anchorage Daily News more | enlarge Chuck Pratt swims the Lynx Lake loop in Nancy Lakes State Recreation Area earlier this month. The route took in 15 lake swims in 14 lakes -- starting and ending swimming the same lake -- and 14 portages. Pratt had a support canoe paddled by Mark Wedekind and Pratt's daughters, Samantha, 9 (middle) and Hannah, 11. title: Hiker creates a unique adventure
summary: Photo courtesy of Kevin Vig enlarge Palmer's Kevin Vig hiked each race route in the Alaska Mountain Running Grand Prix plus a 7.5-mile hike to Exit Glacier consecutively. His journey spanned 60 miles and 23,400 vertical feet -- about 3,000 feet more than the height of Mount McKinley. It took him 24 hours, 45 minutes to tackle the Government Peak Climb, Matanuska Peak Challenge, Mount Marathon, Exit Glacier to Harding Ice Field, Bird Ridge and the Crow Pass Crossing. title: A family cycling dynasty emerges
summary: BILL ROTH / Anchorage Daily News more | enlarge Jessica Yeaton, 15, and her brother Josh Yeaton, 17, competed as part of a team in the 24 Hours at Kincaid last month.
